prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my dear, a CPU is like the little brain inside your computer or phone, you see. It’s what makes everything work—like how your brain tells your body what to do, the CPU tells the computer what to do. It does all the thinking and calculations, very fast, so you can play games, watch videos, or even talk to me right now! Some CPUs are stronger than others, just like how some people are better at puzzles than others. And when it gets too hot, it might need a little rest, just like Grandpa after raking the leaves! Isn’t that interesting?
